description_long: This is a wooden cabin with a complex, multi-sectioned roof structure, featuring exposed rafters and beams. The main section of the cabin has a large, steeply pitched hip roof, while an adjoining section extends with a partially open framework, suggesting ongoing construction or a partially covered veranda. Multiple large windows with dark frames are distributed across the visible facades, allowing ample natural light into the interior. The cabin sits on a raised platform supported by visible posts, and the wood texture is consistent throughout, giving it a rustic and natural appearance. The overall design suggests a blend of traditional craftsmanship and open, airy spaces, suitable for a residential or retreat setting.
description: A rustic wooden cabin with a steep hip roof, exposed beams, and large windows, featuring a partially open side structure. The design blends traditional woodwork with modern, airy elements.
description_view_0: View 0 shows the cabin from above and slightly to the side, highlighting the intersecting rooflines, exposed rafters, and the partially open framework on the right.
description_view_1: View 1 presents the front corner of the cabin, emphasizing the main hip roof, large corner windows, and the covered porch area.
description_view_2: View 2 provides a top-down perspective, clearly displaying the full roof layout, the open framework on one side, and the overall footprint of the structure.
description_view_3: View 3 shows the cabin from the side, focusing on the long facade with multiple windows and the transition between the main roof and the open framework.
attribute_roof_type: hip roof with intersecting gable extension, exposed rafters, and deep overhangs, constructed from wood
attribute_window_arrangement: multiple large rectangular windows, asymmetrically distributed with clusters on the front and side facades, including a prominent corner window grouping
attribute_door_placement: one main door visible on the side section beneath the open framework, adjacent to the window cluster
